dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 for incident at Through Rd, Apt 702, reported fall.
Hello, my name is Mark, and I'm an EMT with the alliance squad. What made you call 911 today?
I can't tell, really. I seem to have a terrible headache and feel dizzy... also I've been vomiting.
When did these symptoms start and what brought it on?
I was lying in bed and I just suddenly felt a terrible headache, and then I got really dizzy and started to feel sick.
Has anything in particular brought on this feeling since then? Anything that improves or worsens it?
I don't get any better, and it's gotten worse since you arrived. Otherwise, I just feel generally icky.
Can you tell me your name, please?
Yes, my name is Bernice.
What county are we in right now?
Riverside, I think?
What is today's date?
August the 13th, I believe.
Can you explain why you called 911 today?
I was in bed and I suddenly started getting this terrible headache, dizziness and nausea.
Just to clarify, you're A&O x4-alert, oriented to person, place, time, and situation. That's correct?
That sounds about right. I'm not confused from what I can tell.
Hi Bernice, can you tell me about any past medical conditions or current medications you may have?
I have COPD, gout, osteoarthritis, asthma, high blood pressure, heart disease, and I've had a stroke.
Are you taking any medications daily? Any reported allergies?
I can't remember what I'm currently on. But I do have allergies to penicillin and azithromycin.
What did you last eat or drink before this began?
I had some tea around 8 p.m. the night before.
Do you feel any weakness on one side of your body or notice trouble with your speech or vision?
No, not really. I felt weakness attempting to walk to the rollator, but it wasn't one-sided.
Thank you, Bernice. Do you have any questions or concerns for us so far?
Not really. I'm just waiting for the ambulance to do its thing and get me to the hospital.
On a scale of 1 to 10, with 10 being the worst possible, how would you rate your headache?
Honestly about a 4.
Does anything seem to trigger or make the pain worse?
A lot of things intensify it: lying still or moving too quickly.
No signs of airway obstruction observed at this time.
Go ahead and check her pulse and respiration for me, okay?
Pulse rate is 101-strong and regular.
Respirations are at 18 per minute. Regular and unlabored breathing noted.
Blood pressure is 150 over 71.
Oxygen saturation is at 97 percent.
She is not pale or diaphoretic. Skin color and appearance are unremarkable.
Do you feel any pain or stiffness in your head or neck?
No stiffness and no neck pain. I had a very mild ache at the back of my head.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam; Partner
dialogue.utterance: We found yellow emesis outside the ambulance area. No blood in nasal or oral cavity.
Bernice, is there any discomfort or pain in your chest?
No, not really. I know my chest can be on fire with COPD, but not now.
We're going to palpate and check your abdomen for tenderness or distention now.
Okay.
General abdominal discomfort reported by the patient. No rigidity or tenderness on palpation.
No signs of deformities, angulations or wounds in the extremities or back.
Glucose was not recorded at this point. Simultaneous lab testing upon hospital admittance is required.
Bernice, we're going to start an IV for you in case we need to give fluids or medications.
IV was started in the extremity-successful first try.
We'll be doing a 4-lead ECG and a 12-lead ECG for heart assessment and abnormal rhythm checks.
ECG report shows sinus rhythm with a long QT interval.
12-lead ECG completed, showing a prolonged QT interval. Reassess in an hour.
Respiratory rate noticed at 16 breaths per minute.
How is the nausea feeling right now? On a scale of 0 to 10?
Probably around 7 at the moment.
I'm going to give you Zofran now to help alleviate the nausea. Any objections?
Please go ahead. Just make the nausea better.
Zofran 4mg IV administered-nausea improved.
Pulse is 86-some improvement noted.
Respiration rate is now 16 per minute.
Blood pressure reading is now 160 over 67.
Glucose taken now-169 mg/dL recorded.
Oxygen saturation still at 97 percent.
Decision made for protocol medical-Nausea and Vomiting.
Second 12-lead ECG done. Long QT interval still recorded.
EKG stable with sinus rhythm and long QT-no cardiovascular signs yet.
Pulse is 74 now.
Respirations now at 15 per minute.
Blood pressure is now 153 over 78.
Oxygen saturation has dropped to 87 percent.
We'll be transporting you to [COMPANY_NAME]-Forest for emergency care.
Transferring to rollator by stand and pivot, then to stretcher using straps and rails.
We're securing you now for the ride. Any specific preferences for positioning?
Either way, I just want comfort.
I see that you had some difficulty trying to wander in the house. How are your legs doing now?
I have more strength now, but didn't have weakness on one side before.
You are A&O x4 still-aware of your surroundings and situation.
I can talk clearly and understand you well.
ID card readied for handoff.
Just to clarify, do you have any recent or current concerns about possible coronavirus symptoms or exposures?
No. I haven't been feeling ill beyond this episode today. No cough or fever. No close contacts recently that I'm aware of.
You will be in ED #7 at the hospital. We believe the primary concern to be headache, dizziness, and nausea.
I feel too unwell. Just get it done safely and quickly.
We will be right with you until the ED receives you. There's no rush.
GCS score of 15-fully alert and coherent observed throughout our time.
Just confirming again, no report of coronavirus symptoms or recent travel or logged exposure.
No signs of stroke or cognitive impairment noted.
